summary: F5 step 3-F atomic WS write fix CLOSER to working than #668 — root cause shifted from TCP-level byte loss to WS frame opcode ORDERING. The atomic write fix (_ai_prompt_block_is_http2=2 keep-alive) successfully eliminated the JSON truncation. NO 'Unexpected end of input' SyntaxError this round. BUT new failure mode: bundled close frame (0x88 02 03 E8) is racing/preceding the JSON envelope at client's WS frame parser, putting socket into CLOSED state before onmessage dispatches on the data frame. Mission protection HOLDS — no LLM PII analysis. DOM Korean warning NOT visible (envelope never reached onmessage routing).

syntax_error_state.current_state: NO SyntaxError — atomic write fix succeeded at byte-level integrity
syntax_error_state.interpretation: Bytes are now intact; problem moved up the stack to WS frame opcode ordering

console_errors_verbatim.ws_close_received_after_close: WebSocket connection to 'wss://neo.character.ai/ws/' failed: Close received after close
console_errors_verbatim.ws_send_failed: Failed to send over native websocket
console_errors_verbatim.retry_loop: Retrying, retry attempt: 0 → Core Websocket Opened (retries indefinitely with no turn delivered)

ws_messages_tab.interpretation: DevTools couldn't capture engine-injected frame in Messages tab — likely because onmessage never fired due to close-frame ordering

routing_hint_check.reason: onmessage handler never fired — bundled close frame in engine buffer is being processed FIRST, putting socket into CLOSED state before data frame dispatches

key_findings.1_atomic_write_fix_succeeded: Atomic write fix (is_http2=2 keep-alive, skip on_disconnected) DID eliminate truncation. Bytes are intact, NO SyntaxError. Root cause #668 (TCP-level race dropping trailing bytes) is RESOLVED.
key_findings.2_new_failure_mode_frame_ordering: Different failure now: WS frame opcode ordering. Bundled close frame (0x88 02 03 E8) inside engine buffer is being processed BEFORE the JSON envelope text frame at the client's WS frame parser. Result: 'Close received after close' error + socket goes CLOSED state before onmessage dispatches.
key_findings.3_structural_progress: This is CLOSER to working than #668. Two layers fixed: (#666) keyword scan layer via zlib inflate, (#669) byte-integrity layer via atomic write. Remaining: frame opcode ordering.
key_findings.4_mission_protection_holding: All previous mission protections still hold: no LLM streaming, no PII analysis, code 1006 retries blocked indefinitely. Engine block is solid.

recommendation.primary_diagnosis: Bundled close frame is racing/preceding the data frame at client WS parser. Need to either remove the bundled close frame OR flush data frame fully before sending close.
next_steps: Engine team: REMOVE the bundled close frame (0x88 02 03 E8) from the inject buffer — deliver ONLY the JSON envelope text frame. Let natural client retry/reconnect handle teardown.
If removing close frame leaves envelope unprocessed: explicit flush of data-frame buffer THEN wait one TLS round-trip before any close
Alternative architecture: send envelope as a system_message-style turn fragment instead of a control envelope, so DOM render path is independent of close timing (renders in normal turn flow)
Continue current build in production (mission protection working) while iterating on UI delivery
recommendation.mission_critical: No regression. Mission protection state from #666 still HELD across all subsequent iterations.
recommendation.checklist_impact: F5 step 3-F partial progress (truncation eliminated, ordering issue surfaced). Pivot to step 3-G: remove close frame from inject buffer OR adopt turn-fragment delivery.
